So after LEFT:
- Player moved from col 20-21 to col 15-16
- Target moved from col 22 to col 14
Both the player and the target moved LEFT together. The yellow target is now at col 14, player at col 15-16. They are very close. Let me try moving LEFT again to get to the target.
Wait, actually looking at the boundaries:
- Player: [[37,15], [38,16], [39,16], [40,15]]
- Target: [[38,14], [39,14]]
The target is at col 14, player is at col 15-16. So the player is to the RIGHT of the target. Moving LEFT would bring the player closer to the target.
But wait - both moved left together. That means the whole scene is scrolling, not the player is moving relative to the scene. Let me think about this differently.
Actually, looking at the image, the player (blue) is on the left side of the blue area. The yellow target is to the left of the player. If LEFT moves everything left (scrolling), then the player is moving right relative to the scene.
Hmm, let me reconsider. Maybe LEFT moves the player leftward on the board. The target also moved left, which means either:
1. The whole scene scrolls when the player moves
2. The target follows the player
Let me try moving LEFT again to see if the player reaches the target.
